current_paradigm.idea: Gompertz-logistic hybrid with x1-dependent rate and half-time using log1p for stability
current_paradigm.description: Replace the Richards model with a Gompertz-logistic hybrid: y = c5 * exp(-exp(-c0*(log_t - c2))) + c6, where log_t = log(x0 + exp(c7)) and c0, c2 depend on x1. The Gompertz function is asymmetric by construction (faster rise, slower approach to plateau) and is widely used in biological growth modeling. Rate: c0 = c1 * exp(c8 * x1) (exponential dependence on x1 for positive-only rate). Half-time: c2 = c3 + c4 * log(x1 + 1) using log1p-style shift for x1≥0 safety. This gives 7 constants with a cleaner parameterization. The Gompertz avoids the Richards shape parameter while naturally capturing the asymmetric S-curve seen in nucleation-dominated aggregation.
current_paradigm.what_to_optimize: Asymmetric sigmoidal datasets where Richards shape parameter c4 is far from 1; robustness when x1 values include 0
current_paradigm.cautions: exp(-exp(...)) is doubly-nested exponential; ensure the inner exp doesn't overflow by bounding c0*(log_t - c2) to reasonable range via good initial values. Use c0_init=1, c2_init=8, c7_init=5.6 to match current best.

active_paradigms.idea: Double-sigmoid (biphasic) kinetics model replacing single Richards sigmoid
Gompertz-logistic hybrid with x1-dependent rate and half-time using log1p for stability
Richards model with x1-modulated time offset instead of separate rate and half-time terms

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
"""Symbolic regression for multi-dataset protein aggregation kinetics.

Richards (generalised logistic) model with log-time using a positivity-
guaranteed time offset: log(x0 + exp(c7)).

Key insight: using exp(c7) as the time offset guarantees the log argument
is always positive (exp(c7) > 0 always), preventing the optimizer from
finding invalid solutions where log(x0 + c7) becomes undefined. With
c7_init=5.6, exp(5.6)≈270 > 256.6 = |x0_min| for the most negative
dataset (Y...

current_paradigm.idea: Richards model with x1-modulated time offset instead of separate rate and half-time terms
current_paradigm.description: Simplify the current model by collapsing the rate and half-time x1-dependence into a single x1-scaled log-time: log_t_scaled = log(x0 + exp(c6)) - c0*log(x1 + c1). This means the effective time is shifted by x1 concentration, which is equivalent to time-concentration superposition (a well-known principle in polymer/aggregation kinetics). Expression: c4 / (1 + exp(-c2 * (log(x0 + exp(c6)) - c0*log(x1+c1) - c3)))^c5 + c7. This uses 8 constants but with a more physically principled x1 interaction. The log(x1+c1) term with fitted c1>0 ensures positivity for any x1. This is equivalent to the WLF/time-temperature superposition principle applied to concentration.
current_paradigm.what_to_optimize: Datasets where concentration acts as a time-scaling factor (time-concentration superposition); reduce parameter redundancy between rate and half-time x1 terms
current_paradigm.cautions: log(x1+c1) requires c1>0 during fitting; use c1_init=1.0. The superposition principle may not hold for all datasets but the model degenerates gracefully (c0→0 gives no x1 dependence). Ensure c5 (Richards shape) initial value is 1.0.

active_paradigms.idea: Double-sigmoid (biphasic) kinetics model replacing single Richards sigmoid
Gompertz-logistic hybrid with x1-dependent rate and half-time using log1p for stability
Richards model with x1-modulated time offset instead of separate rate and half-time terms

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
"""Symbolic regression for multi-dataset protein aggregation kinetics.

Richards (generalised logistic) model with log-time using a positivity-
guaranteed time offset: log(x0 + exp(c7)).

Key insight: using exp(c7) as the time offset guarantees the log argument
is always positive (exp(c7) > 0 always), preventing the optimizer from

dataset (Y...

current_paradigm.idea: Double-sigmoid (biphasic) kinetics model replacing single Richards sigmoid
current_paradigm.description: Many protein aggregation datasets show biphasic or multi-step kinetics (nucleation + elongation phases). Replace the single Richards sigmoid with a sum of two logistic terms sharing log-time but with independent rates and half-times. Expression: c5/(1+exp(-c0*(log_t - c2)))^c4 + c6/(1+exp(-c1*(log_t - c3)))^c8 + c7. This captures both fast and slow aggregation phases. Use 9 constants. The two terms can degenerate to a single sigmoid if c6→0, making it backward-compatible. Both terms use log(x0+exp(c9)) for the same guaranteed-positive time offset. x1 enters through the half-times: half1 = c2 + c3*log(x1+1), half2 = c2 + c3*log(x1+1) + c_offset, so x1 shifts both phases together.
current_paradigm.what_to_optimize: Datasets with biphasic kinetics that the single sigmoid underfits; mean NMSE across all 60 datasets
current_paradigm.cautions: Two-sigmoid models can have identifiability issues if both terms are identical; use different initial values for the two half-times (e.g., 6 and 10) to break symmetry. Keep total constants under 13. Watch for overflow in exp terms.

active_paradigms.idea: Double-sigmoid (biphasic) kinetics model replacing single Richards sigmoid
Gompertz-logistic hybrid with x1-dependent rate and half-time using log1p for stability
Richards model with x1-modulated time offset instead of separate rate and half-time terms
